response: Originating from 1960s U.S. defense research and the 1969 debut of ARPANET, the internet evolved through foundational milestones like the 1983 adoption of TCP/IP and Tim Berners-Lee’s 1989 invention of the World Wide Web. The 1990s launch of accessible browsers and the volatile dot-com era established enduring tech giants, while later innovations like cloud computing and the 2007 iPhone shifted the network toward mobile, mainstream use. What began as a limited academic and military project has ultimately transformed into a ubiquitous global infrastructure. Today, it connects over 5 billion people and fundamentally underpins nearly every aspect of modern commerce, communication, and daily life.
